ai_insights.executive_summary: The NWMS network delivered mixed results in February 2026, with an average Performance Score of 72.6 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average). Closing-time compliance improved markedly across four clinics, yet we continue to see variability in scheduling discipline and nurse-to-chair efficiency that warrants attention. Gig Harbour stands out as the highest performer at 79/100, while Puyallup and Olympia each registered 68–69/100 and present distinct operational challenges.
highlights: Tacoma reduced treatments running past closing from 0.8 per day in January to 0.3 in February—a 60.5% improvement—while also lowering minutes past close per patient from 26.1 to 14.5 minutes across the same period. Federal Way achieved an even sharper reduction in closing-time overruns, with minutes past close per patient dropping 61.3% from 42.2 in January to 16.3 in February, alongside a 15.0% gain in nurse utilization from 58.0% to 66.7%, demonstrating tangible progress in operational discipline.
concerns: We noticed Puyallup's iAssign utilization has declined consistently over three months to 42.1%—roughly half the company average of 86.4%—and this underadoption may be associated with the clinic's elevated minutes past close per patient (49.2 minutes, roughly double the company benchmark of 26.4 minutes). Similarly, Olympia's chair utilization remains elevated at 80.0%, and despite improvements in the past four months, the clinic is averaging 3.2 treatments past closing daily—nearly three times the network typical of 1.1—suggesting capacity constraints and scheduling friction that merit exploration of assignment workflow and template alignment.
recommendations: Tacoma presents the clearest performance gap: average patient delay of 8.5 minutes exceeds the company average of 6.68 minutes by 27%, even as the clinic's closing-time compliance has improved sharply. We recommend auditing Tacoma's scheduling templates and appointment-spacing intervals against iOptimize recommendations to identify whether current slot definitions are allowing adequate buffer time; this targeted review may unlock further gains in on-time starts without operational strain.

ai_insights.executive_summary: NWMS delivered solid operational performance in March 2026, with a network-wide Performance Score average of 71.8 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), outpacing the baseline. Four of five clinics scored above 70, and we saw meaningful month-over-month improvements across patient flow metrics: Federal Way's average delay dropped 43.7% from 3.4 minutes in February to 1.9 minutes in March, while Gig Harbour's average delay fell 29.9% from 5.5 to 3.9 minutes over the same period. However, one clinic requires targeted attention to bring patient experience fully in line with network standards.
highlights: Federal Way continues to lead the network, with a Performance Score of 74 and scheduler compliance of 97.1%—well above the company average of 88.2%—while also achieving the lowest average patient delay at 1.9 minutes. Gig Harbour demonstrated exceptional month-over-month progress, with average delay improving from 5.5 minutes in February to 3.9 minutes in March (a 29.9% reduction) and minutes past close per patient dropping from 15.4 to 7.4 minutes (a 52.0% improvement), earning a Performance Score of 82. Both clinics show strong scheduler discipline and iAssign adoption, suggesting their operational playbooks merit closer study.
concerns: Puyallup presents a distinct outlier: iAssign utilization stands at 4.5%, roughly 94.1% below the group mean of 80.1%, indicating minimal adoption of our assignment algorithm despite a four-month declining trend (3-month average: 47.3%). We noticed this clinic also carries a higher treatment-past-closing rate of 0.9 daily compared to the network average of 1.0, and scheduling compliance of 84.0% trails peers like Federal Way and Tacoma. Olympia warrants attention on patient delays: at 8.4 minutes, it runs 51% higher than the company average of 5.58 minutes, and this metric has declined over three consecutive months (3-month average: 7.6 minutes), alongside the highest treatments-past-closing rate at 2.8 daily—a signal worth exploring together.
recommendations: We recommend Olympia conduct a targeted audit of nurse-to-chair assignment workflows and real-time schedule adherence to address the 51% delay gap versus the company average. The combination of elevated average delay (8.4 minutes), highest closing-time overruns (2.8 treatments daily), and above-average nurse utilization (78.1%) suggests potential friction between chair capacity and nursing throughput; a structured review of how iAssign recommendations (currently at 88.2%) align with actual staffing levels may surface bottlenecks that modest scheduling adjustments could resolve.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
